 {"id":"ft-og6q6.2.4","title":"[replay][T1.4] Enforce redaction, sensitivity classification, and retention boundaries","description":"Apply redaction and sensitivity policies during artifact capture so replay bundles preserve determinism-relevant semantics without leaking secrets or violating retention constraints.

## Deliverables

### CaptureRedactor
- CaptureRedactor struct with configurable redaction rules
- Operates on RecorderEvent stream between capture and serialization
- Sensitivity tiers: T1 (standard, safe for CI), T2 (internal, team access), T3 (restricted, incident-only)
- Redaction modes: mask (replace with block chars), hash (one-way SHA-256), drop (remove field entirely)
- Token patterns: API keys (sk-*, AKIA*, ghp_*, xoxb-*), JWT tokens, connection strings, bearer tokens
- Custom patterns via redaction_rules.toml config file

### SensitivityClassifier
- Classifies each RecorderEvent field into sensitivity tier
- IngressText.content: scan for token patterns, T2/T3 if found, else T1
- EgressOutput.content: same scanning
- Detection.matched_text: inherit tier from source event
- PolicyDecision.context: always T2 minimum (may contain internal details)
- Classifier is deterministic: same input always gets same classification

### RetentionEnforcer
- Enforces per-tier retention limits at artifact write time
- T1: 90 days default, T2: 30 days, T3: 7 days (all configurable)
- Expired artifacts are tombstoned (header preserved, content replaced with REDACTED_EXPIRED)

## Test Requirements

### Unit Tests (>=30)
- Each token pattern correctly detected
- Mask mode replaces with correct-length replacement string
- Hash mode produces deterministic SHA-256 replacement
- Drop mode removes field entirely, preserving event structure
- T1/T2/T3 classification for each RecorderEventPayload variant
- Sensitivity inheritance for Detection events
- Mixed-tier event: highest tier wins
- Redaction is idempotent
- Redacted events still pass schema validation
- Custom patterns from redaction_rules.toml loaded correctly
- Retention enforcer tombstones expired T3 artifacts
- Unicode content redaction preserves valid UTF-8 boundaries

### Proptest Properties (P-06 through P-08 from taxonomy)
- Redaction stability: redacting same event twice yields identical output
- Redacted events still deserialize and pass schema validation
- No original secret text appears in redacted output

### E2E Test Script: tests/e2e/test_replay_redaction.sh
- Scenario 1: Capture with known API key in pane output, verify masked in .ftreplay
- Scenario 2: Set sensitivity to T1, verify T2/T3 fields dropped
- Scenario 3: Set retention to 0 days for T3, verify tombstoning
- Scenario 4: Roundtrip redacted artifact, verify no data loss in non-redacted fields
- Structured JSON logging: {"test":"redaction","scenario":N,"secrets_found":N,"secrets_redacted":N,"tier":"T1|T2|T3","status":"pass|fail"}

## User Impact
- Pain removed: Operators cannot safely share replay artifacts because they may contain secrets. This bead makes artifacts safe-by-default.
- Measurable outcome: Zero secret leaks in T1 artifacts (verified by automated secret scanning in CI).
- No-regression: Existing recorder_audit.rs A0-A4 tiers unchanged; capture redaction is an additional layer.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Sensitive fields are classified and redacted deterministically.

 {"id":"ft-og6q6.2.5","title":"[replay][T1.5] Add artifact chunking, compression, hashing, and integrity validation","description":"Implement the complete .ftreplay v1 artifact writer and packaging pipeline.

## Writer Implementation (core deliverable)
Assemble captured RecorderEvent records, entity metadata, and decision records into the 5-section .ftreplay format per docs/replay-ftreplay-schema-v1.md:
- Section marker emission (--- ftreplay-header/entities/timeline/decisions/footer ---)
- Header generation with capture metadata, content counts, sensitivity tier, integrity checksums
- Entity section: pane and session entities from capture context
- Timeline section: events in strict RecorderMergeKey order, one JSONL line per event
- Decisions section: detection/workflow/policy decisions with definition hashes
- Footer generation with verification metadata and invariant report
- SHA-256 integrity checksums computed per-section and embedded in header

## Chunking (for traces >100K events)
- Chunk boundary logic with configurable chunk size
- Per-chunk .ftreplay files, each independently valid
- Manifest file (trace.manifest.json) with chunk ordering and cross-chunk causality references

## Compression
- gzip and zstd support with auto-detection on read via file magic bytes
- Extensions: .ftreplay.gz, .ftreplay.zst
- Compression applied to entire file, not per-section

## Integrity Validation
- SHA-256 checksum verification on read (compare header checksums against computed values)
- Event count, decision count, entity count validation against header
- Merge order spot-check (first/middle/last) or full verify

## Deliverables
- FtreplayWriter struct with streaming write API (add_entity, add_event, add_decision, finalize)
- FtreplayValidator for read-time integrity checking
- Comprehensive unit tests (>=30) covering writer roundtrip, chunking edge cases, compression roundtrip, integrity violation detection
- e2e test script: tests/e2e/test_replay_artifact_write_read.sh with structured JSON logging

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Deliver full scope for this bead without regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, boundary/edge behavior, and explicit failure/error handling.
3. Add deterministic integration/end-to-end scenarios including at least one failure injection and one recovery path.
4. Emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code) and persist triage artifacts.
5. Document run commands and evidence locations and wire them into normal validation/CI paths.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Artifacts support chunked write/read for large runs.

 {"id":"ft-og6q6.2.6","title":"[replay][T1.6] Implement artifact reader, schema migrator, and compatibility checks","description":"Build artifact loader with schema-version compatibility checks and migration hooks so old captures remain usable as format evolves without corrupting deterministic semantics.

## Deliverables

### ArtifactReader
- ArtifactReader::open(path) -> Result<ReplayArtifact> validates magic bytes, parses header, checks schema version
- Supports: raw .ftreplay, gzip .ftreplay.gz, zstd .ftreplay.zst
- Streaming mode: ArtifactReader::stream_events(path) -> impl Iterator<Item=Result<RecorderEvent>> for large artifacts
- Validates footer checksums against computed SHA-256 of timeline + decisions
- Returns ArtifactError::IntegrityMismatch if checksums fail

### SchemaMigrator
- Registry of Migration trait objects: fn migrate(event: Value) -> Result<Value>
- Version chain: v1->v2, v2->v3, etc., migrations compose automatically
- Each migration is a pure function (no side effects)
- migrate_artifact(artifact, target_version) -> Result<MigrationReport>
- MigrationReport: events_migrated, fields_added, fields_removed, warnings

### CompatibilityChecker
- check(artifact_version, engine_version) -> Compatibility enum
- Compatibility: Exact, MigrationAvailable(chain), Incompatible(reason)
- Pre-flight check before replay: Incompatible refuses with clear error
- MigrationAvailable migrates in-memory (original file untouched)

## Test Requirements

### Unit Tests (>=30)
- Open valid v1 artifact, parse all sections
- Open gzip and zstd compressed artifacts
- Reject wrong magic bytes, truncated header, mismatched checksums
- Stream 10K-event artifact without loading all into memory
- Schema v1->v2 migration adds new field with default, preserves existing
- Migration chain v1->v3 composes correctly
- Migration is idempotent
- CompatibilityChecker returns correct Compatibility variants
- Error messages include file path and version info
- Empty timeline/decisions sections parse without error

### Proptest Properties (P-14, P-15 from taxonomy)
- Any valid artifact written can be read back with identical event sequence
- Migration chain produces valid schema-conformant output

### E2E Test Script: tests/e2e/test_replay_artifact_reader.sh
- Scenario 1: Write then read artifact, verify identical events
- Scenario 2: Corrupt 1 byte in timeline, verify IntegrityMismatch
- Scenario 3: Create v1 artifact, migrate to v2, verify parseable
- Scenario 4: Read artifact with unknown future version, verify Incompatible error
- Structured JSON logging: {"test":"artifact_reader","scenario":N,"events_read":N,"version":"v1","compression":"none|gzip|zstd","integrity":"pass|fail","status":"pass|fail"}

## User Impact
- Pain removed: As .ftreplay format evolves, old regression artifacts become unreadable. This bead ensures backward compatibility.
- Measurable outcome: All v1 artifacts remain readable after any future schema evolution.
- No-regression: Existing WAR playback (replay.rs) unchanged; new reader for .ftreplay format.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Reader validates and reports schema version compatibility.

## Deliverables

### Integration Test Suite
- tests/replay_capture_integration.rs:
  I-06: Full capture pipeline: ingest event -> capture adapter -> redaction -> compression -> artifact write -> artifact read -> verify identical events
  I-07: Decision capture: trigger pattern match during capture, verify DecisionEvent in artifact
  I-08: Redaction roundtrip: capture with secrets, read back, verify secrets masked
  I-09: Schema migration: write v1 artifact, migrate to v2, read back, verify all events intact
  I-10: Large artifact: 100K events, verify streaming reader handles without OOM

### Structured Logging Harness
- All capture pipeline tests emit JSON logs per test taxonomy
- Log fields: capture_events, decisions_captured, secrets_detected, secrets_redacted, compression_ratio, read_events, roundtrip_match
- Artifacts retained in target/test-artifacts/capture/

### E2E Test Script: tests/e2e/test_replay_capture_pipeline.sh
- Scenario 1: Simulate ft watch session, capture to .ftreplay, verify artifact structure
- Scenario 2: Capture with mixed sensitivity data, verify T1 output is secret-free
- Scenario 3: Write large artifact, verify gzip compression ratio >50%
- Scenario 4: Full roundtrip: capture -> write -> read -> replay -> verify decisions match
- Summary: {"test":"capture_pipeline","scenarios_pass":N,"scenarios_fail":N,"total_events":N,"roundtrip_match":true|false}

## User Impact
- Pain removed: Without pipeline integration tests, individual components may work but the full capture path can silently lose or corrupt data. This suite verifies the complete chain.
- Measurable outcome: Zero data loss in capture pipeline (verified by roundtrip equality checks).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.

 {"id":"ft-og6q6.3.1","title":"[replay][T2.1] Implement virtual clock and deterministic scheduler core","description":"Implement the virtual clock and deterministic scheduler core for the replay kernel.

## Virtual Clock
- VirtualClock struct that replaces wall-clock time with trace-driven time
- Time advances ONLY when the replay scheduler processes the next event
- Speed control: 1x (wall-clock), 2x (double speed), instant (no delays)
- Clock provides occurred_at_ms and recorded_at_ms for the current event

## Deterministic Scheduler
- ReplayScheduler consumes events from the .ftreplay timeline section
- Events are processed in strict RecorderMergeKey order (5-key total order)
- For each event, the scheduler feeds it to the appropriate engine (pattern, workflow, policy)
- Side effects are isolated (no real I/O, no real pane mutations)
- Scheduler state is serializable for checkpoint/resume support

## Key Invariant
Two runs of the same trace with the same code produce byte-identical decision sequences (EQ-06, EQ-07 from equivalence contract).

## Deliverables
- VirtualClock with speed control API
- ReplayScheduler with event-driven processing loop
- ReplayConfig struct (speed, filters, equivalence level)
- Comprehensive unit tests (>=30): clock advancement, speed modes, ordering verification, determinism proof (replay same trace twice, compare)
- e2e test script: tests/e2e/test_replay_kernel_determinism.sh that captures a trace, replays it twice, and verifies byte-identical decision output

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Deliver full scope for this bead without regressions in adjacent behavior.

 {"id":"ft-og6q6.3.2","title":"[replay][T2.2] Build stable event ordering and pane merge resolver","description":"Build the stable event ordering and pane merge resolver for multi-pane replay.

## Event Ordering
- Implement RecorderMergeKey-based total ordering for multi-pane event streams
- Merge resolver accepts N pane streams and produces a single ordered output stream
- Verify ordering satisfies EQ-07 (identical merge key ordering between runs)
- Handle concurrent events (same recorded_at_ms) via deterministic tiebreaking

## Pane Merge Resolver
- PaneMergeResolver maintains per-pane iterators over timeline entries
- Produces events in globally sorted order using a priority queue keyed by RecorderMergeKey
- Handles gap markers as first-class events (no skipping)
- Detects and marks clock anomalies per the ClockAnomalyTracker contract

## Deliverables
- PaneMergeResolver with streaming merge API
- Clock anomaly detection and marking during merge
- Comprehensive unit tests (>=30): single-pane ordering, multi-pane merge, concurrent event tiebreaking, gap marker ordering, clock anomaly detection
- Proptest: merge output is always sorted regardless of input pane order (P-10)
- e2e test script: tests/e2e/test_replay_merge_ordering.sh verifying multi-pane traces produce identical ordering across runs
